critical.name: jq
critical.why: All 17 hooks require jq for JSON I/O — no fallback

critical.name: python3
critical.why: dbq CLI uses match statements, walrus operator, type unions (PEP 604)

critical.name: git
critical.why: Session hooks, version control, commit workflow, dirty-tree detection

important.name: sqlite3
important.why: Direct DB queries, session_briefing.sh, backup operations

important.name: git-config
important.why: First commit will fail without user identity configured
important.fix: git config --global user.name 'Your Name' && git config --global user.email 'you@example.com'

templates.note: Templates are bundled in the bootstrap engine — no separate setup required
templates.why: Templates are source files that bootstrap_project.sh copies into new projects

frameworks.why: Session protocol, phase gates, delegation, correction protocol, etc. Bundled locally in v1.0.

engine.description: The dbq CLI and supporting scripts bundled in each generated project
engine.note: Copied from bootstrap engine at activation time — no global dependency

darwin: bash ships as 3.2 — brew install bash for 5.x
date -j flag differs from GNU date -d
linux: sed -i has no '' argument (handled by sedi() helper)

host.application: Claude Code (CLI, Desktop App, or IDE extension)
host.plan_note: Claude Max plan recommended for multi-model orchestration (Opus for architecture, Sonnet for implementation)
host.note: Host requirements are informational — cannot be machine-checked from a hook
